title: Pega Constellation UI Architecture — Complete Guide
content:
# Pega Constellation UI Architecture — Complete Guide ## What is Constellation? Constellation is Pega's modern UI architecture that replaces the traditional UI (also called Classic or Legacy UI). It is a React-based, component-driven front-end framework built on Pega's DX API (Digital Experience API). Constellation was introduced in Pega Infinity 8.7 and is the default UI architecture starting from Pega Infinity 23. ### Key Differences: Constellation vs Traditional UI - **Traditional UI**: Uses harnesses, sections, and auto-generated layouts. Tightly coupled to the Pega rules engine. Customization through skin rules and custom CSS. - **Constellation**: Uses a modern React-based component library. Decoupled front-end communicating via DX API. Standardized design system with consistent UX. Limited but intentional customization through design templates. ## Constellation Design System Constellation provides a comprehensive design system with pre-built components: ### Core Components - **Fields**: Text input, dropdown, radio buttons, checkboxes, date pickers, autocomplete, URL, email, phone, currency, integer, decimal, text area, rich text editor - **Layouts**: Stacked, Inline, Grid, SimpleTable, Details (wide/narrow), DefaultForm, SubTabs - **Templates**: Page templates (Single page, Multi-step, Wide-narrow), Case templates, Portal templates - **Widgets**: Assignment list, Case summary, Feed/Pulse, Attachment, SLA indicator, Followers ### Design Tokens Constellation uses design tokens for consistent theming: - Typography: Font family, sizes, weights, line heights - Colors: Primary, secondary, neutral, semantic (success, warning, error, info) - Spacing: Based on 4px/8px grid system - Border radius, shadows, z-index layers ### Theming Deep Dive Constellation theming is controlled through the application definition and design tokens, NOT through CSS skins like Traditional UI: - **Application-level theme**: Set in Application Definition → Theme tab. Choose a base theme (Light or Dark) and override primary/secondary colors. - **Brand colors**: Primary color drives buttons, links, and active states. Secondary color is used for accents and highlights. - **Component-level tokens**: Each component type has its own token set. For example, Button has tokens for background, text color, border, hover state, disabled state, and focus ring. - **Custom themes**: Create custom themes by extending the base theme and overriding specific tokens. Export/import themes as JSON for reuse across applications. - **Dynamic theming**: Constellation supports runtime theme switching (e.g., light/dark mode toggle) without page reload. - **Theme inheritance**: Child applications inherit the parent application's theme unless explicitly overridden. - **Contrast ratios**: All default themes meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast requirements. ## Architecture Overview ### DX API (Digital Experience API) Constellation communicates with the Pega server exclusively through the DX API: - RESTful API endpoints under `/api/application/v2/` - JSON-based request/response format - Stateless design — no server-side session dependency for UI rendering - Supports CRUD operations on cases, assignments, data objects - Key endpoints: - `POST /cases` — Create new case - `GET /cases/{ID}` — Get case details - `PUT /assignments/{ID}` — Submit assignment action - `GET /data_views/{ID}` — Query data views - `GET /casetypes` — List available case types - `GET /assignments/{ID}/actions/{actionID}` — Get action details - `POST /data_views/{ID}` — Query data with parameters - `GET /pages/{pageID}` — Get data page contents ### DX API Authentication The DX API supports multiple authentication methods: - **OAuth 2.0**: Recommended for production. Supports Authorization Code, Client Credentials, and ROPC flows. - **Basic Auth**: Username/password. Suitable for development and testing only. - **Token-based**: Use Pega's token service endpoint (`/PRRestService/oauth2/v1/token`) to obtain access tokens. - **CORS configuration**: Configure allowed origins in Pega Application Definition → Security tab for cross-origin DX API calls. ### Component Architecture ``` Pega Server (Rules Engine) ↕ DX API (REST/JSON) Constellation UI Layer ├── DX Component Bridge ├── React Component Library ├── State Management (Redux-based) ├── Design System (Tokens + Theme) └── Rendered UI (Browser) ``` ### How Constellation Renders a Form 1. User navigates to a case/assignment 2. DX API returns a JSON metadata response describing the view 3. Constellation's DX Component Bridge maps metadata to React components 4. React renders the components with appropriate design tokens 5. User interactions trigger DX API calls to the server 6. Server processes rules and returns updated metadata ### Metadata-Driven Rendering Constellation's rendering is entirely metadata-driven. The server sends JSON metadata that describes WHAT to render, and the client decides HOW to render it: - **View metadata**: Describes the layout structure, fields, and their arrangement - **Field metadata**: Property name, type, label, validation rules, visibility conditions, read-only state - **Action metadata**: Available actions (submit, save, cancel), button labels, confirmation messages - **Navigation metadata**: Breadcrumbs, tabs, step indicators for multi-step processes - This decoupling means the same metadata can be rendered by different front-end implementations (React, Angular, Web Components, mobile) ## Constellation Portals ### Portal Types Constellation provides several portal configurations: - **Full Portal**: Complete application portal with navigation, worklist, and case management. Used for internal business users and case workers. - **Embedded Portal**: Lightweight portal designed to be embedded within another application or website. No chrome/navigation — just the case flow. - **Self-Service Portal**: Customer-facing portal for external users to create and track their own cases. Simplified navigation and branding. - **Mashup**: Embed a single Constellation case or view into an external web page using the Pega Mashup SDK. ### Portal Configuration - **Navigation**: Configure left sidebar navigation items (worklist, dashboard, case types, custom pages) - **Landing page**: Set the default landing page users see after login - **Branding**: Logo, application name, favicon, loading screen - **Access control**: Control which case types and features are available per role - **Multi-portal**: An application can have multiple portals for different user types (e.g., agent portal vs customer portal) ## Constellation Configuration in App Studio ### Enabling Constellation 1. Navigate to Application → Definition → Application form 2. Under 'UI Architecture', select 'Constellation' 3. Save and run the application 4. Note: Once switched to Constellation, switching back requires careful testing ### Creating Views in Constellation Views in Constellation are configured in App Studio: 1. Open a case type in App Studio 2. Navigate to the workflow step 3. Click on the step to configure the view 4. Use the view editor to add/remove/arrange fields 5. Select the appropriate template (form, details, confirmation, etc.) ### Page Templates - **Single page**: All content on one scrollable page - **Multi-step (Wizard)**: Step-by-step workflow with navigation - **Wide-narrow**: Two-column layout with primary content and sidebar - **Landing page**: Dashboard-style template with widgets ### View Templates - **DefaultForm**: Standard data entry form with stacked fields - **Details**: Read-only view with labeled field values - **ListView**: Tabular data display with sorting and filtering - **Confirm**: Confirmation/summary view after case creation - **NarrowPage**: Compact view for sidebar panels - **CaseView**: Complete case view with tabs for details, attachments, history, followers - **DataReference**: Display and manage linked data objects ### Repeating Structures & Dynamic Layouts Constellation handles repeating/dynamic data through: - **SimpleTable layout**: Tabular display of page list properties with add/edit/delete row capabilities. Configure columns, sorting, and filtering in App Studio. - **FieldGroupList**: For complex repeating groups with multiple fields per row. Each row can expand to show a detail form. - **Dynamic references**: Use data references to link to other case types or data types. Constellation auto-generates the appropriate UI for creating/selecting referenced items. - **Embedded pages**: Properties of type Page or Page List render as nested forms within the parent view. Configure which properties of the embedded page appear using the view editor. - **Pagination**: Large lists automatically paginate. Configure page size in the view template settings (default: 10, 25, 50, 100 rows). ## Constellation Customization ### Design Templates (Recommended) The primary way to customize Constellation is through design templates: 1. Create a custom design template in Dev Studio 2. Template is a React component that follows Constellation patterns 3. Register the template so it appears in App Studio 4. Apply the template to views in App Studio ### Custom DX Components For advanced customization: 1. Use the Constellation DX Component Builder SDK 2. Build custom React components 3. Package and deploy to Pega application 4. Components must follow Constellation design system guidelines 5. SDK provides: React framework, Pega bridge APIs, testing utilities ### What You Cannot Customize in Constellation - Core navigation chrome (top bar, sidebar) - Overall page structure beyond template selection - Direct CSS overrides (no custom skins like Traditional UI) - Server-side HTML generation (everything is client-side React) ## DX Component Builder SDK — Deep Dive The DX Component Builder SDK is the primary tool for building custom Constellation components. ### SDK Setup 1. Install prerequisites: Node.js 18+, npm 9+ 2. Download SDK from Pega Marketplace or your Pega instance (Dev Studio → Configure → Application → Distribution → DX Component Builder) 3. Initialize project: `npx @pega/custom-dx-components create my-component` 4. Project structure: - `src/components/` — React component source files - `src/components/MyComponent/index.tsx` — Component entry point - `src/components/MyComponent/config.json` — Component metadata and property definitions - `src/components/MyComponent/demo.stories.tsx` — Storybook stories for testing ### Component Lifecycle - **getPConnect()**: The primary bridge API. Returns a PConnect object that connects the component to the Pega server. - **getDataObject()**: Retrieve data from Pega clipboard - **setValue(propertyRef, value)**: Update a property value on the clipboard - **getActions()**: Access available actions (submit, cancel, etc.) - **refreshComponent()**: Force re-render with latest server data - **getCaseInfo()**: Get current case context (ID, status, urgency, etc.) ### Component Types You Can Build - **Field components**: Custom input controls (e.g., signature pad, color picker, map picker, barcode scanner) - **Template components**: Custom page or view layouts (e.g., dashboard layout, kanban board, timeline view) - **Widget components**: Custom widgets for case summary, analytics, embedded content - **Utility components**: Wrappers for third-party libraries (charts, maps, rich editors) ### Building a Custom Field Component — Example ```jsx import { useEffect, useState } from 'react'; export default function StarRating(props) { const { getPConnect, label, value, readOnly } = props; const pConn = getPConnect(); const [rating, setRating] = useState(value || 0); const handleClick = (newRating) => { if (!readOnly) { setRating(newRating); pConn.setValue('.Rating', newRating); } }; return (
{[1, 2, 3, 4, 5].map(star => ( handleClick(star)} style={{ cursor: readOnly ? 'default' : 'pointer', color: star <= rating ? 'gold' : 'gray' }}> ★ ))}
); } ``` ### Component config.json Structure ```json { "name": "StarRating", "label": "Star Rating", "description": "A 5-star rating input component", "type": "Field", "subtype": "Custom", "properties": [ { "name": "label", "label": "Label", "format": "TEXT" }, { "name": "value", "label": "Value", "format": "INTEGER" }, { "name": "readOnly", "label": "Read Only", "format": "BOOLEAN" } ] } ``` ### Deploying Custom Components 1. Build: `npm run build` — compiles and bundles the component 2. Package: `npm run package` — creates a ZIP file for deployment 3. Upload to Pega: Dev Studio → Import → select the packaged ZIP 4. Component appears in App Studio's component palette for use in views 5. Version management: Increment version in config.json before each deployment ### Testing Custom Components - **Storybook**: SDK includes Storybook for isolated component testing. Run `npm run storybook` to launch. - **Mock PConnect**: SDK provides a mock PConnect for unit testing without a Pega server. - **End-to-end**: Test deployed components in a Pega development environment before promoting. ## Pega Cosmos React (Open-Source Design System) Pega Cosmos React is Pega's open-source design system library, available on npm as `@pega/cosmos-react-core` and related packages. While Constellation uses Cosmos internally, the Cosmos library is also available for building standalone applications or custom portals. ### Cosmos React Packages - `@pega/cosmos-react-core`: Core UI components (buttons, inputs, modals, cards, etc.) - `@pega/cosmos-react-work`: Work-specific components (case summary, worklist, assignment) - `@pega/cosmos-react-condition`: Conditional rendering utilities - `@pega/cosmos-react-social`: Social/collaboration components (feed, pulse, followers) ### Using Cosmos in Custom Components When building DX components, use Cosmos components for consistent look and feel: ```jsx import { Button, Card, Flex, Text } from '@pega/cosmos-react-core'; export default function MyWidget() { return ( My Custom Widget ); } ``` ### Cosmos Design Principles - **Accessibility first**: All Cosmos components meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards - **Responsive**: Components adapt to screen size using Pega's responsive breakpoints - **Themeable**: Components consume design tokens for consistent theming - **Composable**: Small, focused components that compose into complex UIs ## Constellation SDKs for External Applications Pega provides SDKs for building Constellation-compatible UIs outside the Pega portal: ### React SDK (Constellation React) - Build standalone React applications that connect to Pega via DX API - Full component library matching Constellation's built-in components - Available on GitHub: Pega Constellation React SDK - Use cases: custom customer portals, mobile web apps, embedded case views - Setup: `npx @pega/react-sdk-components create my-app` ### Angular SDK - Angular implementation of Constellation components - Same DX API integration, Angular-native component architecture - Supports Angular 14+ - Available on GitHub: Pega Constellation Angular SDK ### Web Components SDK - Framework-agnostic implementation using native Web Components - Can be used with any JavaScript framework or vanilla JS - Ideal for embedding Pega UI in existing applications regardless of tech stack - Shadow DOM encapsulation prevents style conflicts ### SDK Common Features - Pre-built authentication flows (OAuth 2.0) - Automatic form generation from DX API metadata - Built-in validation and error handling - Offline support capabilities - Responsive layouts matching Constellation design system ## Accessibility in Constellation Constellation is built with accessibility as a core requirement: ### Built-in Accessibility Features - **Keyboard navigation**: All components are fully keyboard accessible. Tab order follows logical reading order. Focus indicators are visible and meet contrast requirements. - **Screen reader support**: ARIA labels, roles, and live regions are built into every component. Form fields have proper label associations. Error messages are announced automatically. - **Color contrast**: All color combinations meet WCAG 2.1 AA (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text). The default theme has been audited for compliance. - **Focus management**: When dialogs open, focus moves to the dialog. When dialogs close, focus returns to the trigger element. Page navigation announcements for screen readers. - **Reduced motion**: Respects `prefers-reduced-motion` media query. Animations are subtle and can be disabled. - **RTL support**: Right-to-left text direction support for Arabic, Hebrew, and other RTL languages. - **High contrast mode**: Compatible with Windows High Contrast Mode. ### Accessibility Testing - Pega runs automated accessibility tests on every Constellation component release - Use browser extensions (axe, WAVE) to test custom components - Test with actual screen readers (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver) - Verify keyboard navigation for all interactive elements ## Constellation vs Traditional UI: Migration Guide ### When to Use Constellation - New applications built on Pega Infinity 23+ - Applications requiring modern, responsive UI - Teams wanting standardized, consistent UX - Applications leveraging DX API for headless/multichannel ### When to Stay on Traditional UI - Heavy custom UI with complex skins/CSS - Applications using custom HTML/JavaScript sections - Embedded Pega UI in iframes with custom chrome - Legacy applications where migration cost exceeds benefit ### Migration Steps 1. **Assessment**: Catalog all custom UI elements (skins, sections, harnesses, custom controls) 2. **Identify gaps**: Determine which customizations have Constellation equivalents 3. **Rebuild views**: Recreate views using Constellation templates in App Studio 4. **Custom components**: Build DX components for functionality with no standard equivalent 5. **Test thoroughly**: Validate all user flows, responsiveness, accessibility 6. **Parallel run**: Run both UIs in parallel if possible during transition ### Common Migration Challenges - Custom skins → Design tokens and templates - JavaScript-heavy sections → DX Component SDK - Custom controls → Constellation field types or custom DX components - Complex harnesses → Page templates + composite views - Direct DOM manipulation → React component patterns - Custom event handlers → PConnect bridge API callbacks - Server-side JavaScript (activities with UI) → DX API calls + client-side logic ### Migration Mapping: Traditional → Constellation - Harness → Page template - Section → View - Skin → Theme / Design tokens - Custom control → DX Component - Layout (Group, Grid, Dynamic Layout) → Constellation Layout (Stacked, Inline, Grid) - Tab layout → SubTabs template - Tree grid → ListView with expand/collapse - Modal dialog → Overlay/Modal component - Cell within layout → Region within template ## Constellation Performance Optimization ### Client-Side Performance - **Lazy loading**: Constellation lazy-loads components that are off-screen or in collapsed sections. Views in non-active tabs are loaded on-demand. - **API call optimization**: DX API uses ETags for caching. Unchanged data returns 304 Not Modified. Batch API calls where possible. - **Bundle size**: Use code splitting for large custom component packages. Tree-shaking removes unused Cosmos components. - **Virtualization**: Large lists use virtual scrolling — only visible rows are rendered in the DOM. ### Server-Side Performance - **Data pages**: Use data page caching strategies (node-level, requestor-level, thread-level) to reduce server load. - **View optimization**: Minimize properties on views — only include fields that users need. - **Prefetch**: Configure data page prefetch for views that need data ready on load. - **Pagination**: Always paginate large data sets. Never load unbounded lists. ## Constellation Troubleshooting ### Common Issues **View not rendering / blank page** - Check browser console for JavaScript errors - Verify DX API is accessible: `GET /api/application/v2/` - Check if Constellation is properly enabled on the application - Clear browser cache and service workers - Verify user has proper access to the case/assignment **Fields not appearing on form** - Check view configuration in App Studio - Verify field is mapped to a property on the case class - Check visibility conditions on the field - Ensure the property exists and has correct type - Check if the field is on the correct view (some steps have multiple views — confirm, review, etc.) **Styling/theme issues** - Constellation doesn't support custom CSS/skins - Use design tokens for theming - Check theme configuration in Application Definition - Verify design template is properly registered - Clear CDN/browser cache after theme changes **DX API errors** - `401 Unauthorized`: Check authentication token/session - `403 Forbidden`: Verify user access rights - `404 Not Found`: Check endpoint URL and case/assignment ID - `422 Validation Error`: Check required fields and data types - `500 Server Error`: Check Pega logs (PegaRULES log, Alert log) **Custom DX Component Issues** - Component not appearing in App Studio palette: Verify config.json is valid, check component type/subtype, reimport the package - Component renders but data not updating: Check getPConnect().setValue() calls, verify property references start with '.' - Styling conflicts: Use Cosmos design tokens instead of custom CSS. If custom CSS needed, scope with CSS modules or unique class prefixes - Build errors: Verify Node.js version (18+), clear node_modules and reinstall **Performance issues** - Large forms: Break into multi-step wizard - Too many API calls: Check for unnecessary refreshes - Slow rendering: Review component complexity - Use browser DevTools Performance tab to identify bottlenecks - Check Network tab for slow DX API calls and optimize data pages ### Debugging Constellation 1. **Browser DevTools**: Network tab to inspect DX API calls, Console for errors 2. **Pega Tracer**: Trace server-side rule execution 3. **DX API Tester**: Test API calls directly (use Postman or browser) 4. **Console logs**: Check for React component errors 5. **Clipboard Viewer**: Inspect data on the clipboard 6. **React DevTools**: Install React DevTools browser extension to inspect component hierarchy, props, and state 7. **Redux DevTools**: Inspect Constellation's internal state management ## Constellation Best Practices 1. **Start with templates**: Always begin with standard Constellation templates before customizing 2. **Use App Studio**: Configure views in App Studio, not Dev Studio, whenever possible 3. **Follow design system**: Adhere to Constellation's design patterns for consistency 4. **Minimize custom components**: Use standard components and only build custom when necessary 5. **Test accessibility**: Constellation components are accessible by default — don't break this 6. **Responsive design**: Constellation is mobile-first — test on multiple screen sizes 7. **DX API first**: If building external/headless UIs, use DX API directly 8. **Version compatibility**: Check component availability for your Pega version 9. **Performance**: Keep views lean — avoid overloading forms with too many fields 10. **Theme consistently**: Use design tokens across all custom components 11. **Use Cosmos components**: When building custom DX components, use @pega/cosmos-react-core for consistency 12. **Test with screen readers**: Verify custom components work with NVDA/JAWS/VoiceOver 13. **Batch API calls**: Minimize round trips to the server by batching related DX API calls 14. **Cache data pages**: Set appropriate caching on data pages used by Constellation views 15. **Document custom components**: Include README and Storybook stories with every custom DX component ## Constellation Interview & Certification Q&A ### Frequently Asked Questions **Q: What is the difference between Constellation and Cosmos?** A: Constellation is the full UI architecture and framework within Pega that renders case management UIs. Cosmos is the underlying design system and component library that Constellation uses. Think of Cosmos as the building blocks (buttons, inputs, cards) and Constellation as the assembled application (portals, views, case management flows). Cosmos React is also available as an open-source npm package for use outside Pega. **Q: Can you use Constellation and Traditional UI in the same application?** A: Yes, but with limitations. An application is set to either Constellation or Traditional at the application level. However, you can have mixed architectures across different applications in the same system. Some organizations run a Constellation-based customer portal alongside a Traditional-based internal tool. **Q: How does Constellation handle offline scenarios?** A: Constellation SDKs (React, Angular) support offline capabilities through service workers and local caching. The DX API supports ETag-based caching. However, full offline case processing requires additional configuration and is not available out-of-the-box in all versions. **Q: What happens to existing custom controls when migrating to Constellation?** A: Existing custom controls (autocomplete, custom dropdowns, etc.) from Traditional UI do NOT work in Constellation. They must be rebuilt as DX Components using the DX Component Builder SDK. There is no automatic migration path for custom controls. **Q: Can Constellation render without a Pega server?** A: The Constellation SDKs (React, Angular, Web Components) can render a UI shell without a live Pega server, using mock data for development. However, in production, a Pega server with DX API is required for case management, data access, and business logic execution. **Q: What is the minimum Pega version for Constellation?** A: Constellation was introduced in Pega Infinity 8.7. It became the default UI architecture in Pega Infinity 23 (also called Pega Infinity '23). For the best experience and full feature set, use Pega Infinity 23 or later. **Q: How do you handle complex validation in Constellation?** A: Constellation supports validate rules, edit validate rules, and when conditions for field-level and form-level validation. Client-side validation runs first (required fields, format checks), then server-side validation runs on submit. Custom validation logic can be added through DX Components using the getPConnect().validate() API.
